subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: Reactivity with water increases on moving down the Goup from $\mathrm{Li}$ to $\mathrm{Cs}$ as $\mathrm{Li} < \mathrm{Na}$ $ < \mathrm{K} < \mathrm{Rb} < \mathrm{Cs}$. This is due to increase in electropositive character. Hence Rb reacts most vigorously with water.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
options: Both A and R are true but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
solution:
Mg have 2 electrons in valance shell and after donation of 2 electrons it converts into Mg2+ ion and attains inert gas configuration, now to ionize one more electron from inert gas configuration is very difficult, hence it is easy to ionize Mg to Mg2+.  IE2 is always greater than IE1 because on removal of e-, size decreases and effective nuclear charge increases.So Assertion is correct.

Mg2+ is a smaller ion than Mg+ and has a greater charge density. This means that Mg2+ has a stronger attraction to its electrons than Mg+ and requires more energy to remove an electron from it. So reason is also correct.

chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|l|l|}\hline & B & Al & Ga & In & Tl \\\hline \begin{array}{l} \text{Atomic radius} \\(\mathrm{pm})\end{array} & 88 & 143 & 135 & 167 & 170 \\\hline \text{Electronegativity} & 2 & 1.5 & 1.6 & 1.7 & 1.8 \\\hline \begin{array}{l} \text{Density} \\\left(\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{cm}^3\right)\end{array} & 2.35 & 2.7 & 5.9 & 7.31 & 11.85 \\\hline \begin{array}{l} \text{Ionisation} \\Energy (kJ/mol)\end{array} & 801 & 577 & 579 & 558 & 589 \\\hline\end{array}$
Radius Order $\quad \mathrm{T} \ell \gt \mathrm{In} \gt \mathrm{A} \ell \gt \mathrm{Ga} \gt \mathrm{B}$
EN Order $\mathrm{B} \gt \mathrm{T} \ell \gt \mathrm{In} \gt \mathrm{Ga} \gt \mathrm{Al}$
Density Order $\mathrm{T} \ell \gt \mathrm{In} \gt \mathrm{Ga} \gt \mathrm{A} \ell \gt \mathrm{B}$
$\mathrm{IE}_1$ Order $\mathrm{B} \gt \mathrm{T} \ell \gt \mathrm{Ga} \gt \mathrm{A} \ell \gt \mathrm{In}$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
(i) True, $\mathrm{T} \ell^{+}$is more stable than $\mathrm{T} \ell^{3+}$, due to inert pair effect. So $\mathrm{T} \ell^{3+}$ is a powerful oxidising agent.
(ii) True, $\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Al}^{3+/ A 1}}^0=-1.66 \mathrm{~V}$. So it is difficult to reduce $\mathrm{A} \ell^{3+}$. So $\mathrm{A} \ell^{3+}$ is highly stable.
(iii) False, as $\mathrm{T} \ell^{3+}$ is unstable
(iv) True, $\mathrm{T} \ell^{+}$is more stable than $\mathrm{T} \ell^{3+}$
(v) True, $\mathrm{A} \ell^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{T} \ell^{+}$are highly stable

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
(i) due to lanthanide contraction $\mathrm{T} \ell$ has more I.E. as compared to $\mathrm{Ga}$ and $\mathrm{A} \ell$
(ii) due to scandide contraction Ga has more I.E. as compared to $\mathrm{A} \ell$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
Electron gain enthalpy is the amount of energy released when an element accepts an electron. The process is endothermic.

correct order of electron gain enthalpy is  O<S>Se>Te

⇒ S>Se>Te>O

(Oxygen shows least electron gain enthalpy due to small size of atom)

solution: In a period from left to right atomic size decreases.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: The linking of identical atoms with each other to form long chains is called catenation. However, this property decreases from carbon to lead. Decrease of this property is associated with $\mathrm{M}-\mathrm{M}$ bond energy which decreases from carbon to lead.

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution:
In a period the value of ionisation potential increases from left to right with breaks where the atoms have some what stable configuration. After removing an $e^{-}$ oxygen will acquire stable configuration. Hence $\mathrm{O}^{+}$has highest second ionisation enthalpy than $\mathrm{F}^{+}$and $\mathrm{N}^{+}$.
$\therefore$ correct order is $\mathrm{O}>\mathrm{F}>\mathrm{N}>\mathrm{C}$

subject: Chemistry
chapter: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
solution: In the third period, Al comes after Mg and before Si. Since in Mg (1s2 2s2 2p6 3s2 ), the last electron is to be lost from a fully filled 3s-orbital while in Al (ls2 2s2 2p6 3s2 3p1 ) it is to be lost from a 3p-orbital, therefore, ∆iH1 of Al is lower than that of Mg (737 kJ mol-1). Further, nuclear charge increases in going from Na to Al, therefore ∆iH1 of Al is higher than that of Na(496 kJ mol-1) but lower than that of Mg (737 kJ mol-1). Therefore, the appropriate value of ionisation potential of  Al is 577 kJ mol-1.
